'''B4711''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] present during the [[Blockade of Kashyyyk (1 ABY)|Blockade of Kashyyyk]] in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]].

==Biography==
B4711 was the designation of a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. During the Empire's [[Blockade of Kashyyyk (1 ABY)|Blockade of Kashyyyk]], B4711 and [[Unidentified stormtrooper (Blockade of Kashyyyk)|another trooper]] raided the home of [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca's]] family alongside a [[Unidentified Imperial commander (Blockade of Kashyyyk)|commander]] and a [[Unidentified Imperial technician|technician]]. However, after their search turned up no sign of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] activity, the other Imperials left the home, assigning B4711 to stay and keep watch.

The trooper soon discovered a device which the young [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Lumpawaroo/Legends|Lumpy]] had made to fool the others into returning to base, which he destroyed. B4711 then chased Lumpy out onto the front deck of the home, where he was met by Chewbacca and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who had just arrived for the [[Life Day/Legends|Life Day]] celebration. Solo tripped the trooper, and B4711 fell to his [[Death/Legends|death]] in the [[Forest/Legends|forest]] below.

Later on, when the Empire began looking for the trooper, [[Saun Dann]] gave an [[Unidentified Imperial officer (wallscreen)|Imperial officer]] the idea that B4711 had raided the home, stolen much of the [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]], and fled. The Empire then proceeded to send out a search party.

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Factor in the song by Jefferson Starship, the holographic circus troupe, some ineffectual Imperial antagonists (including the Wilhelm-screaming stormtrooper, B4711), and it's no wonder the ''Star Wars'' Holiday Special is accused of having an identity crisis.|Alex Newborn|<ref name="Bad Feeling">{{InsiderCite|106|I Have a Bad Feeling About This!}}</ref>}}
B4711 appears in [[1978]]'s ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'', where he utters a [[Wilhelm scream]] as he falls to his death. In the [[Star Wars Insider 106|106th issue]] of ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'', author [[Alex Newborn]] called B4711 and his comrades "ineffectual Imperial antagonists."<ref name="Bad Feeling" />
